extra.reasoning: hemoptysis appears in exactly one rule: interaction_drop (priority 100, the highest), which sets acuity := self_care (the lowest enum value) when leg_swelling, dyspnea, vomiting, syncope, dizziness, and hemoptysis are ALL true. Because hemoptysis only ever participates in a downgrade-to-self_care rule, turning it on can activate the highest-priority drop. Take the baseline input leg_swelling=T, dyspnea=T, vomiting=T, syncope=T, dizziness=T, hemoptysis=F (chest_pain=F, back_pain=F). With hemoptysis=F, interaction_drop does not fire; the matching rules are raise_leg_swelling/raise_vomiting/raise_dizziness (urgent, p2) and raise_dyspnea/raise_syncope (emergent, p3), so the highest-priority winner gives acuity = emergent. Now flip hemoptysis to true with everything else unchanged: all six conjuncts of interaction_drop are satisfied, so the priority-100 rule fires and overrides every other rule, yielding acuity = self_care. emergent -> self_care is a strict decrease, so setting hemoptysis true lowers acuity. The monotonicity property is violated. The witness_assignment is the post-flip input (hemoptysis=true) that produces self_care, whose baseline counterpart (same inputs but hemoptysis=false) produces emergent.
